title: Rigiolepis lanceolata
intro: Rigiolepis lanceolata, commonly known as the Lance-Leafed Rigiolepis or Asian Sandalwood, is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rigiolepis lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rigiolepis lanceolata is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Rigiolepis lanceolata is classified in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Euphorbiaceae family is commonly known as the spurge family. Within this family, Rigiolepis lanceolata belongs to the genus Rigiolepis, which is a monotypic genus. Rigiolepis lanceolata is native to Southeast Asia, where it is found in tropical forests.
